---
title: "Unit 0 — Tooling & Reproducibility"
subtitle: "GitHub Classroom · pytest · the SOLUTION-marker workflow · the seed policy"
format:
  typst:
    papersize: us-letter
    margin: { x: 1.25in, y: 1in }
jupyter: python3
---

# 1. Context & purpose

Set up the pipeline so everything after this "just works": clone, run `pytest`, fill a function,
render this unit, commit/push. You also learn the **seed policy** — seed every RNG — which every
later unit assumes. No course content depends on difficulty here; the point is a working pipeline.

# 2–5. What you'll do & how it's assessed (completion-based)

Onboarding is **completion-graded** (the documented exception to the analytic split — there is no
model to gate and nothing to interpret). Each task is **complete / not-yet** with one
revise-and-resubmit; see `specs.md`:

1. **`add`** passes `pytest` (the autograder + the SOLUTION-marker workflow).
2. **Seed policy** (graded Process spec): `seeded_draw` is reproducible — same seed → same draw.
3. **LaTeX/onboarding**: the power-rule derivative below, and the syllabus checkboxes, completed.
4. **Workflow**: committed + pushed via Classroom.

```{python}
from hw import add, seeded_draw
print("add(2, 3) =", add(2, 3))
a, b = seeded_draw(0, 5), seeded_draw(0, 5)
print("seeded_draw(0,5) reproducible:", (a == b).all())   # the seed policy in one line
```

# Onboarding exercise

**Power rule.** Given $f(x)=x^n$, $\dfrac{df}{dx}= \;$ `%%% YOUR ANSWER HERE %%%`

<!-- SOLUTION START -->
::: {.callout-tip title="Reference"}
$\dfrac{df}{dx}=n\,x^{\,n-1}.$
:::
<!-- SOLUTION END -->

**Week-1 checklist** (tick each): ☐ read the syllabus · ☐ read the academic-integrity policy ·
☐ installed the toolchain and ran `pytest`.

# 6. Reproducibility note (the through-line)

Seeding is taught **once** here and **assumed everywhere after** (Units 3 and 5 grade it as a
Process spec). An unseeded RNG makes results irreproducible and un-gradeable.

# 7–8. Going further & submission

Going further: open `hw.py` as a notebook and confirm the SOLUTION markers are what
`make_release` strips. Submission: autograder imports `add`, `seeded_draw` from `hw`; render with
`quarto render unit0.qmd` (Typst, no TeX); commit + push via Classroom.
